scenario: A team using Self-Managed Camunda 8 needs to manage users / groups / permissions. The SaaS edition has Console / Identity SaaS; Self-Managed uses a different mechanism.
question: What identity provider does Self-Managed Camunda 8 typically use?

options.text: Camunda Identity is available in Self-Managed deployments, not only in SaaS. The Identity component is a core part of the Self-Managed Camunda 8 stack and provides user management, group management, permission assignment, and OAuth2 / OIDC token issuance. Self-Managed installations ship with a packaged Keycloak instance by default for Identity management. SaaS uses Camunda's hosted Console for equivalent functionality, but both deployment models have identity management capabilities.

User identities and permissions do not live inside BPMN models. BPMN models may reference user identity concepts such as candidate groups or assignees in User Task configuration, but the actual user accounts, group memberships, and permissions are stored and managed in the Identity provider (Keycloak in Self-Managed deployments). BPMN process definitions reference identity information by name or expression; the actual identity data is maintained externally in the IdP.

md: (1) Management Identity -- handles authn/authz for components outside the Orchestration Cluster (Console, Web Modeler, Optimize); (2) Admin (formerly "Orchestration Cluster Identity") -- handles access to Zeebe, Operate, Tasklist, and the Orchestration Cluster API within each cluster. md:14. Can integrate with external OIDC provider or existing Keycloak instance per :19. 0 -- web login (users) + machine-to-machine (M2M) tokens for applications per :17, :33-38.

Self-Managed Camunda 8 includes a comprehensive role-based access control system through the Identity component. ) and what operations they can perform. Permissions can be assigned via Keycloak roles and groups, and federated from external identity providers such as Azure Active Directory, Okta, or LDAP-based systems. The RBAC model is central to securing production Self-Managed deployments.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 Self-Managed includes an Identity component for user / group / permission management. The default IdP is Keycloak -- a battle-tested open-source IdP. Capabilities: User / group management: create users, assign to groups, manage credentials. OAuth2 / OIDC token issuance: workers and SDKs authenticate via standard OAuth2 client-credentials flow. External IdP delegation: configure Identity to federate with corporate IdPs (Azure AD, Okta, AD via LDAP) for SSO. Permissions: define what users / groups can do in Camunda components (Web Modeler, Operate, Tasklist, etc.). For SaaS: Camunda's Console provides similar functionality hosted by Camunda. Deployment pattern for SM: deploy Identity + Keycloak alongside Zeebe / Operate / Tasklist; configure components to use Identity as auth provider; configure Keycloak (or delegate to external IdP).

c.text: Correct. Users in IdP, BPMN references.
